questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following features is common to both the Arjuna Award and the Dronacharya Award?

options.text: Both were instituted in the same decade to promote sports excellence.
Both awards include a certificate, a cash prize, and a bronze statuette.
Both awards primarily evaluate performance over a fixed four-year window.
Both awards are exclusively given for international medal-winning performances.

questions.explanation: According to the text, winners of the Arjuna Award receive a statuette, certificate, and cash prize. Similarly, Dronacharya awardees receive a bronze statue, certificate, and cash prize. Option A is wrong (1961 vs 1985). Option C is wrong (only Arjuna specifies the 4-year window). Option D is wrong (Arjuna is for 'consistent good performance').
questions.takeaway: While the criteria and timing of institution differ, the physical and monetary components of the Arjuna and Dronacharya awards are similar.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The winners of the Arjuna Award receive a statuette of Arjuna, a certificate and a cash prize... Awardees [of Dronacharya] receive a bronze statue of Dronacharya, a certificate and a cash prize.
